title: Story state (long-form)
description: Machine-read structured memory for a long-form project, at projects/{slug}/novel/state.json. The narrative bible lives in Markdown (bible.md, style-guide.md); THIS file holds the atomic, typed, verifiable facts the continuity gate and chapter-generation consume. Research-grounded: a bible without enforcement is a dictionary nobody consults — so this is structured + gated.

id.description: Stable slug id; referenced by relationships + chapter casts.

died_chapter.description: If status=dead, the chapter number where they died (enables the 'no resurrection' continuity check).

appearance.description: Fixed physical anchors (eye/hair color, height, marks) — the consistency anchors.

rule.description: A hard constraint of the world (magic cost, tech limit, law). Chapters must not violate it.

arcs.description: OPTIONAL arc ids this rule applies to. If omitted/empty the rule is GLOBAL (always injected). If set, build-chapter-context only injects it while writing a chapter inside one of these arcs — lets a long, multi-setting book localize rules so per-chapter context stays small without dropping a rule that's actually in play.

timeline.description: Ordered story events anchored to chapters. Chapter numbers must be non-decreasing.

foreshadowing.description: Planted threads and whether they have paid off. The gate flags resolved-before-setup.

last_advanced_chapter.description: OPTIONAL chapter where this still-open thread was last touched/advanced. build-chapter-context flags a thread as long-dormant when the current chapter is >= --dormant-after beyond this (or setup_chapter if unset) — surfacing planted-and-forgotten threads. It is NOT dropped, only flagged.

arcs.description: OPTIONAL book/arc structure for long works. Bounds what state is 'in play' so chapter-context injection stays small as the book grows (the 60k-tractability lever). If present, every chapter number must fall inside exactly one arc's [start_chapter, end_chapter] range; arcs must not overlap; start_chapters strictly increasing. The last arc may leave end_chapter null (open).

end_chapter.description: Last chapter of this arc; null = still open (only valid on the final arc).

summary.description: One-to-three sentence arc recap (used as arc-level context).

goal.description: What the protagonist is driving at across this arc.

chapters.description: Chapter ledger. number must be unique; cast entries reference character ids.

summary.description: Short recap used as last-N-chapters context.

cast.description: Character ids appearing in this chapter.
